title: blephariglottis lacera
intro: Blephariglottis lacera, also known as the 'Slippery orchid' or 'Mouth orchid,' is a type of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids with reported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Consuming Blephariglottis lacera may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and has also been used to treat digestive problems and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in capsule or tincture form, often used in traditional medicine, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to limited scientific research on its effects.
